event_text: Stephen I is crowned first king of Hungary.
The Golden Bull of 1222 limits royal power and confirms noble privileges.
The Mongol invasion devastates the Kingdom of Hungary.
The Árpád dynasty ends in Hungary.
Matthias Corvinus becomes king of Hungary.
Hungary is defeated by the Ottoman Empire at the Battle of Mohács.
Buda falls to the Ottomans and Hungary is divided into three main parts.
Buda is recaptured from the Ottomans by the Holy League.
Rákóczi's War of Independence challenges Habsburg rule.
The Hungarian Diet accepts the Pragmatic Sanction, recognising Habsburg succession through the female line.
The Hungarian Revolution of 1848 begins in Pest.
Hungarian revolutionary forces surrender at Világos.
The Austro-Hungarian Compromise gives Hungary self-government within the Dual Monarchy.
Buda, Pest and Óbuda unite to form Budapest.
Hungary celebrates its millennium with major events and construction in Budapest.
Hungary fights in the First World War as part of Austria-Hungary.
The Aster Revolution ends Habsburg rule in Hungary.
The Hungarian Soviet Republic briefly takes power.
The Treaty of Trianon redraws Hungary's borders.
Germany occupies Hungary during the Second World War.
The Siege of Budapest ends after heavy fighting.
The Hungarian People's Republic is proclaimed.
The Hungarian Revolution begins in Budapest.
Hungary becomes a democratic republic as communist rule ends.
Hungary joins the European Union.
